response.justification: The candidate is a capable full-stack and polyglot web engineer with a solid track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and distributed web services, but is a poor fit for this role. The single hardest requirement — 8 years of C++ — is essentially absent: the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and a student thesis project, and C++ does not appear in the skills section at all. Cloud compute platform experience is shallow (AWS CDK used as an IaC tool at Optilyz, Kubernetes for a side project), and there is no evidence of the large-scale infrastructure, systems programming, or compute/storage architecture work the role demands. Technical leadership is demonstrated through community workshops and mentoring activities, but not through formal team or architectural leadership at the required scope.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed micro-services (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Consistent community engagement via workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects, showing genuine knowledge-sharing disposition relevant to a mentoring mandate
Broad platform exposure (embedded systems, mobile, web, DevOps tooling, AI-agent infrastructure) reflects adaptability and systems thinking
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI, one thesis project) against an 8-year hard requirement — this alone disqualifies the candidate
No cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure engineering background; AWS usage is limited to CDK/IaC, with no evidence of compute, storage, or network architecture at scale
No formal technical leadership tenure (no team-lead, principal, or architect title with direct reports or cross-team accountability for a platform product)
